SINGLE-DAY TICKETS TO THE 2026 CROSSFIT GAMES NOW AVAILABLE | BUY TICKETS NOW

Wednesday

250618

Workout of the Day

On a 2-minute clock:
25 front squats
Max-calorie Echo bike in the remaining time
Rest 2 minutes

On a 2-minute clock:
20 push presses
Max-calorie Echo bike in the remaining time
Rest 2 minutes

On a 2-minute clock:
15 thrusters
Max-calorie Echo bike in the remaining time
Rest 2 minutes

On a 2-minute clock:
10 squat clean thrusters
Max-calorie Echo bike in the remaining time

Your score is the total number of calories accumulated.

♀ 75-lb barbell
♂ 115-lb barbell

Post total calories to comments.

Stimulus and Strategy:
Today’s workout is four sprint-style efforts. The barbell movements are progressive and build on the previous set as the final set is a combination of the three sets prior. The loading of the barbell should be on the lighter side. Push to complete the barbell movements as quickly as possible to give yourself more time on the bike.

Scaling:
Reduce the load of the barbell.

To reduce the complexity of the barbell movements, perform each movement with a single dumbbell or pair of dumbbells.

In case of an injury or limitation, perform the calories on any machine available. If you have a squatting limitation, perform deadlifts, push presses, hang power cleans, and hang power clean and jerks (instead of the movements in the workout).

Intermediate option:
On a 2-minute clock:
25 front squats
Max-calorie Echo bike in the remaining time
Rest 2 minutes

On a 2-minute clock:
20 push presses
Max-calorie Echo bike in the remaining time
Rest 2 minutes

On a 2-minute clock:
15 thrusters
Max-calorie Echo bike in the remaining time
Rest 2 minutes

On a 2-minute clock:
10 squat clean thrusters
Max-calorie Echo bike in the remaining time

Your score is the total number of calories accumulated.

♀ 65-lb barbell
♂ 95-lb barbell

Beginner option:
On a 2-minute clock:
20 front squats
Max-calorie Echo bike in the remaining time
Rest 2 minutes

On a 2-minute clock:
15 push presses
Max-calorie Echo bike in the remaining time
Rest 2 minutes

On a 2-minute clock:
10 thrusters
Max-calorie Echo bike in the remaining time
Rest 2 minutes

On a 2-minute clock:
5 squat clean thrusters
Max-calorie Echo bike in the remaining time

Your score is the total number of calories accumulated.

♀ 35-lb barbell
♂ 45-lb barbell

Coaching cues:
On the thrusters, focus on keeping the weight glued to your shoulders and then launching the barbell off your body as you stand up out of the squat.

Resources:
The Front Squat
The Push Press
The Thruster
Rogue Echo Bike

Find a gym near you:
View the CrossFit map

Learn the Movement →

Chris Meldrum
June 26th, 2025 at 7:55 pm
Commented on: 250618

As rx'd. 13 / 15 / 11 / 11.


Ouchie.  Unbroken front squats and push press (actually misread and so did push jerks).  Broke thrusters 10-5, which was probably a good decision and also probably a mental cop-out.  Quick singles for the squat clean thrusters.  This one left me on the floor for quite a while.


52m/5'10"/180

Hunter Ferrell

Commented on: 250618

M / 22 / 5'7 / 205.6


Barbell @ 95lbs


Round 1 - I had access to a generic stationary bike, but it wouldn't turn on so I decided to just use it anyway for the first round. I peddled quickly for the remaining time but it was too easy with very little resistence.


Round 2 - Switched to rower / 24 cals


Round 3 - 22 cals


Round 4 - 15 cals


Calories accounted for - 59 cals

Stephan Bucher
June 18th, 2025 at 3:38 pm
Commented on: 250618

Today the squat clean thrusters felt natural and fluid and I possibly could have even added more weight on, but nonetheless happy with progress and completion.

Intermediate scaled to beginner based on weights as follows:

front squats 88lbs (40 kg)

push press 66lbs (30 kg)

thrusters 55lbs (25 kg)

squat clean thrusters 55lbs (25 kg)

total calories on the Echo bike 43

Charles Meyers
June 18th, 2025 at 1:17 pm
Commented on: 250618

Beginner option:

On a 2-minute clock:

20 front squats

Max-calorie Echo bike in the remaining time

8 cal

Rest 2 minutes

On a 2-minute clock:

15 push presses

Max-calorie Echo bike in the remaining time

11 cal

Rest 2 minutes

On a 2-minute clock:

10 thrusters

Max-calorie Echo bike in the remaining time

12 cal

Rest 2 minutes

On a 2-minute clock:

5 squat clean thrusters

12 cal

Max-calorie Echo bike in the remaining time

Your score is the total number of calories accumulated.

♂ 45-lb barbell

got to use my new Assault Bike from Rogue "Awesome"

James Gentile
June 18th, 2025 at 12:47 pm
Commented on: 250618

Rx ish at the Y. No echo bike so did C2 rower calories. Barbell from the ground for all lifts.


10-19-14-13


56 total.


broke the last 3 lifts into 2 sets. FS unbroken but had some top pauses. Hard to get on the rower after the squat clean thrusters! Really fun workout, can pick your spice level, glad I kept the weight at 115 lbs.

intermediate option, Echo bike change with SDHP@20kg.

Comment URL copied!
Comment URL copied!
Lincoln Kerger
June 18th, 2025 at 12:08 pm
Commented on: 250618

Beginner+ (65# BB) on an Echo/Assault style bike by Hammer Strength

25-30-25-29=109cals


High calorie count due to a lot of time on the bike - UB on all movements and they went quick at that weight and volume, but this is Day 5 taper. Will be getting PRP treatment on both knees today, so next couple of weeks will be almost entirely mobility work, no impact cardio work and gradual progression back up, taking longer if needed - healing is the number one goal right now.

Comment URL copied!
Comment URL copied!
Favo K
June 18th, 2025 at 1:41 pm

Good luck today; healing is definitely the priority! Great job today still though!

Comment URL copied!
Comment URL copied!
Jim Rix
June 18th, 2025 at 4:30 pm

Still...you were really moving, bro.

Are your knees hurting from just age and degeneration, or the results of specific injuries? I'll be interested to hear how this treatment works. I'm sure you'll be back and kicking it again soon.

Comment URL copied!
Comment URL copied!
Lincoln Kerger
June 18th, 2025 at 6:53 pm

Thanks you two!


Quadricep tendonosis both knees, Jim. It's probably due to a combination of small injuries over the years, the chronic strain of nearly 3 decades of going up and down stairs and platforms that are continuously moving under me (loaded and unloaded) while still trying to work on fitness, occasionally wearing a bunch of gear while on land, and sprinkling in some age. I've had PRP done on the right elbow with some good results in halting arthritis progression and healing lateral epicondylitis, so I'm hopeful this will work. It just takes resting and a period of significantly reduced activity, both of which I'm not good at adhering to.

Comment URL copied!
Comment URL copied!
Coastie Nick
June 18th, 2025 at 7:30 pm

Great job! I hope the treatment goes well. I can sympathize with that. Sounds like you were describing running on flight decks and ladders during swells. Making the crappy choice between eventually wearing out your knees or sitting in berthing and getting fat on deployment….

Comment URL copied!
Comment URL copied!
Lincoln Kerger
June 18th, 2025 at 9:37 pm

Coastie, yeah, definitely no sitting around anywhere on the ships getting fat no matter what was going on.
